notes: Full vendor eval tables transcribed from the GPT-5.6 GA post (2026-07-09). Scores without effort are the published Sol/Terra/Luna columns (table default; not always max). Rows with effort=ultra are multi-agent. Independent replications may differ. Percent metrics stored as 0–1 accuracy/pass fractions; Elo and AA index scores keep published scale (unit other|elo). Cross-check ARC-AGI-3 effort ladders against arcprize-gpt-5-6-2026-07.json (third-party verified).

scores.caveat: Table value 52.7%. Prose elsewhere on same page cites 53.6 for Sol — keep table as primary.

claims.statement: OpenAI claims Sol sets SOTA on Artificial Analysis Coding Agent Index at 80 with fewer tokens/time/cost vs Fable 5; Terra ~above Fable 5; Luna outperforms Opus 4.8 at ~1/4 estimated cost in their framing. Agents' Last Exam prose cites Sol 53.6 (table 52.7%).
claims.implication: Family tiers (Sol/Terra/Luna) are a first-class cost/quality ladder within one generation.

claims.statement: OpenAI introduces max (beyond xhigh) and ultra (default four parallel agents) as higher-compute operating points that shift score-latency frontiers on BrowseComp, SEC-Bench Pro, Terminal-Bench 2.1.
claims.implication: Effort is multi-step: low…xhigh, plus max/ultra — not a single 3-rung cheap/default/max profile.

claims.statement: API pricing: Sol $5/$30, Terra $2.50/$15, Luna $1/$6 per 1M input/output; cache writes 1.25× uncached input; cache reads 90% discount for GPT-5.6+.
claims.implication: Use pricing/openai-api-2026-07.json for $/MTok; this claim is narrative cross-check only.

claims.statement: OpenAI reports large Sol gains vs GPT-5.5 on ExploitBench (73.5% vs 47.9%), SEC-Bench Pro (71.2% vs 45.8%), ExploitGym (33.7% vs 15.1% peak under longer cap), GeneBench Pro (28.7% vs 12%).
claims.implication: Cyber/science headline gains are vendor-reported; treat as directional until third-party harnesses publish.
